figures.caption: Table 9. Results on StreamBench. We report accuracy(%) on 6 subsets. The best scores are highlighted in bold. ‡ denotes results reproduced by us, while others are taken from prior works. ‘Avg’ denotes the average performance.

Figure 9. Event Merge Prompt Template used in OASIS.

Table 1. Comparison on the OVO-Bench benchmark. We report the accuracy(%) on the Perception and Backward subset. The best results are indicated by bolding. ‡ denotes results reproduced by us, while others are taken from prior works.

Table 2. Comparison on StreamingBench. We report accuracy(%) on All Real-Time Video Understanding (Real-Time All), Misleading Context Understanding (MCU), Anomaly Context Understanding (ACU), and Sequential Question Answering (SQA). † denotes a dynamic frame rate: 1 fps for durations under 5 min, 0.5 fps for 5– 10 min, and 0.2 fps for over 10 min.The best scores are highlighted in bold. ‡ denotes results reproduced by us, while others are taken from prior works.
Figure 7. System Prompt Template used in OASIS.

Figure 2. A critical failure case of prior methods under real-time querying. When asked, “What color are the cars directly in front now?”, the baseline model (top) processes an excessively large context, but its attention is distracted by a previously salient event (the “other car”), causing the relevant signal to be drowned in noise and yielding an incorrect answer. In contrast, OASIS (bottom) adopts an Adaptive Short Context that constrains attention to the most relevant Short Window/Memory, enabling stable grounding and correct prediction.
Figure 5. Visualization of OASIS handling a Real-time Perception query.
Figure 6. Visualization of OASIS handling a Long-term Memory query.
Figure 4. Comparison of Average Token Consumption per query between OASIS and the baseline. The Qwen3-VL-8B uniformly samples video at 0.5 fps.

Figure 3. (a) An overview of OASIS’s multi-resolution event hierarchy and the two-phase reasoning policy that executes adaptive temporal routing. (b) The dynamic merging strategy used for the online and bounded maintenance of the event forest.
